text.title: AI-Assisted Specification Writing Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the ai-assisted-specification-writing methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale. The terminal `skip-this-methodology` rule is the safety valve invoked by the decision tree when preconditions fail.

rule.statement: Author MUST gate at three points: structure, acceptance-criteria, edge-cases — no auto-merge.
rule.rationale: LLM specs miss user-visible edge cases without human gate at AC step.

rule.statement: Output MUST follow `spec.md` template (Summary, Background, Goals, Non-Goals, AC, Edge Cases, Open Questions).
rule.rationale: Free-form specs cannot be parsed by downstream task generators.

rule.statement: Each AC MUST cite product evidence (interview snippet, metric, hypothesis ID).
rule.rationale: Uncited AC drifts into HiPPO territory.

rule.statement: LLM draft MUST stay within `est_tokens` budget; pruning is mandatory.
rule.rationale: Token-bloated specs erode context for downstream stages.

rule.statement: Frontmatter MUST flag `ai_drafted: true` + named reviewer.
rule.rationale: Unflagged AI specs erode review discipline.

rule.statement: Author MUST stop and route elsewhere when the decision-tree resolves to a skip leaf; partial application of this methodology against an unfit input is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Forcing the methodology onto a mismatched input produces low-quality artefacts and erodes trust in the corpus.
